name: BNP Paribas Sustainable Finance Challenge SG 2026
prize: Mentorship from BNP Paribas senior bankers + recognition
summary: Penultimate-year undergrads and postgrads at NTU, NUS, SMU, and Universiti Malaya tackle a real sustainable-finance problem statement curated by BNP Paribas bankers and ESG experts. The 2026 edition includes a mobile-first 'NextGen Sustainable Wealth' brief — design the screens, navigation, ESG analytics, and behavioural nudges for young affluent investors, while staying compliant on greenwashing risk.
eligibility: Penultimate-year undergrads / postgrads at NTU, NUS, SMU, UM. Teams of 3–5 from same university.
highlights: Real, working bank problem statements (not synthetic cases)
Mentored by BNP Paribas senior bankers
Cross-discipline teams encouraged within each university

name: Lee Kuan Yew Global Business Plan Competition
prize: Up to ~S$1M total prize pool across categories
summary: A globally-recognised business plan competition hosted at SMU. Student-led ventures from anywhere in the world compete across multiple stages over months, with judges drawn from ST Engineering, A*STAR, Georgia Tech, and global PE/VC. Heavy emphasis on deep-tech and commercialisable research. Strategic Management author Frank Rothaermel sits on the academic panel.
eligibility: Student-led ventures globally. Multi-stage application.
highlights: One of Asia's largest student business-plan prizes
World-class judging panel from PE/VC and academia

name: Entrepreneur First — Singapore
prize: Up to US$250,000 funding + co-founder matching + global network
summary: A 'talent investor' that backs exceptional individuals before they have a team or an idea. EF runs a structured matchmaking process to pair complementary technical and commercial co-founders, then funds the resulting companies. Strong deep-tech and frontier AI tilt. The Singapore programme connects into EF's portfolio across London, Berlin, Paris, and Bangalore.
eligibility: Solo founders / aspiring founders. Application-based.
highlights: Find a co-founder, then build a company
Global network: LDN, BER, PAR, BLR, SG

name: The FinLab
prize: Pilots with UOB; investor introductions; mentorship
summary: UOB's in-house accelerator, focused on fintech and digital transformation for SMEs across Southeast Asia. Past alum Turnkey Lender (AI-driven lending software) raised a US$2M Vertex Ventures cheque while in the programme. Strong on regulatory navigation, banking partnerships, and SME-distribution channels — areas where most accelerators are weak.
eligibility: Fintech / SME-tech startups. Application-based per cohort.
highlights: Strong on banking regulation and distribution
Sustainability track in addition to core fintech

name: Plug and Play APAC
prize: Corporate pilot programmes + investor network
summary: The Singapore arm of Silicon Valley's prolific accelerator network. Runs 60+ accelerator programmes a year across verticals — brand/retail, fintech, health, supply chain, energy, smart cities. The lever isn't capital; it's structured corporate-pilot matchmaking with multinationals who need startup deal-flow.
eligibility: Vertical-specific. Rolling applications.
highlights: Corporate-pilot matchmaking is the core value

name: BLOCK71
location: Singapore + hubs in US, China, Indonesia
prize: Co-working, mentorship, market access
summary: NUS Enterprise's flagship hub — described as 'the world's most tightly packed entrepreneurial ecosystem.' BLOCK71 has incubated 600+ startups and contributed roughly 25% of Singapore's startup ecosystem valuation. Notable alumni include unicorns Carousell and PatSnap. Less program, more long-running scaffolding: physical co-working, NUS research access, and global market hubs in Suzhou, San Francisco, and Jakarta.
eligibility: Sector-agnostic. Tech-focused founders, often early-stage.
highlights: Hubs in US, China, Indonesia for market access

name: Iterative
prize: US$150K for 10% (YC-style terms)
summary: A Y Combinator-style accelerator for Southeast Asian startups. Founded by ex-YC founders, Iterative invests at pre-seed with standardised terms (US$150K for 10%) and a ruthless focus on getting to product-market fit fast. Cohorts are intentionally small for high-touch mentorship.
eligibility: SEA-based / SEA-targeting startups, pre-seed.

name: Tribe Accelerator
prize: Government-linked partnerships + corporate access
summary: Singapore's first government-supported blockchain accelerator, evolved into a deep-tech programme covering AI and Web3. Sits inside the Startup SG Accelerator scheme — meaning ~SGD 30K operating-expense funding per startup, plus structured access to government agencies that most Web3 founders find impossible to navigate alone.
eligibility: Blockchain / AI deep-tech startups. Cohort applications.
highlights: Corporate partner network in financial services

name: Accelerating Asia
prize: Pre-Series A capital + structured cohort programme
summary: An early-stage VC fund and accelerator targeting pre-Series A startups across Southeast and South Asia. Cohorts get structured curriculum plus check-writing — a hybrid model that's rare in the region. Strong on impact-aligned ventures and unsexy verticals (logistics, agritech, financial inclusion) that pure software accelerators miss.
eligibility: Pre-Series A startups across SEA + South Asia.
highlights: Pre-Series A focus — past pure pre-seed

name: AI Ready ASEAN Youth Challenge 2026
organizer: AI Singapore × ASEAN Foundation × Google.org × IMDA
prize: Regional 1st: US$5,000; Nationals: US$500–200 + paid trip to SG Grand Finals
summary: A regional ideathon spanning 11 ASEAN countries (Brunei, Cambodia, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, Timor-Leste, Vietnam). Participants 18–35 propose practical AI-integrated solutions to real Southeast Asian challenges, aligned with Google.org's focus areas (Knowledge & Skills, Scientific Progress, Stronger Communities). Submissions: 5-page proposal + 3-minute pitch video.
eligibility: 18–35 years old, currently enrolled in an educational institution in one of 11 ASEAN countries.
highlights: Backed by AI Singapore + Google.org + ASEAN Foundation + IMDA
11 ASEAN countries with national + regional rounds
Paid trip to Singapore for national winners

name: ASEAN AI Hackathon 2026
prize: Mentorship + deployment opportunities + industry-academic introductions
summary: The first edition of P2A's regional AI hackathon, themed 'AI for a Resilient ASEAN.' Tracks span Climate Resilience, Public Health & Telemedicine, Smart City & Mobility, and AI for Education. Strong industry-academic bridge: 150+ ASEAN universities in the network, with mentorship and deployment opportunities for winning teams.
eligibility: Active undergrads at P2A-network universities, GPA ≥ 3.0, English CEFR B2+.
highlights: Four practical tracks (climate, health, smart city, edtech)

name: Asian Hackathon for Green Future 2026
organizer: VinUniversity × For Green Future Foundation × VinTechTalent
location: Online preliminaries; finals at VinUniversity, Hanoi
prize: US$24,000 total: 1st US$8,000; 2nd US$5,000; 2× 3rd US$3,000; 5× consolation US$1,000
summary: The first Asia-wide environmental hackathon dedicated to undergraduate and master's students. Three stages: Registration & Preliminary (Apr 6 – May 17), Online Training (Jun 2–28), and Finals at VinUniversity in Hanoi (Jul 2–5). Top 30 of submitted teams advance. Judging panel chaired by Prof Duong Nguyen Vu, formerly NTU Singapore Aerospace.
eligibility: Undergrads + master's students at universities across Asia. Teams up to 4.
highlights: First Asia-wide environmental hackathon for students
Online training phase included between rounds

name: Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon 2026
prize: US$30,000+ prize pool
summary: An MIT-rooted nonprofit running an intense 24-hour AI hackathon spanning 60+ countries. Open-ended challenges in AI automation, healthcare data, intelligent agents, business productivity, and creative AI platforms. Designed to simulate real startup conditions — speed, adaptability, and shippability over theoretical elegance.
eligibility: Anyone of legal age in their country. Beginners through experts.

name: AI Hackathon Series — Built Environment 2026
prize: Awards at ArchiFest 2026 + e-certification
summary: A multi-month hackathon series for the built-environment industry — architects, engineers, and designers using AI to address real practice problems. Format: bi-weekly evening sharing sessions (Feb 25 → May 6) feeding into projects that compete for awards at ArchiFest 2026 in July. Speakers from senior practitioners and AI experts. The May 6 session is the final pre-submission gathering.
eligibility: Built-environment professionals; free to register.
highlights: Bi-weekly sharing sessions with senior practitioners

name: NEXT Hackathon @ SuperAI 2026
prize: AWS credits + sponsor prizes; top 5 demo on SuperAI stage; post-hack acceleration
summary: A 36-hour, application-based AI sprint embedded inside the SuperAI conference. Builders ship from scratch under time pressure with technical mentors physically embedded with each team. Selection is curated — every person in the room is here to ship — and the deck is stacked: full conference access (valued at US$999), free meals, and post-hack acceleration pathways.
eligibility: Application-based. Physical attendance for full 36h required. Teams up to 4. Solo applicants matched into teams.
highlights: 100% free, including SuperAI conference pass valued at US$999
AWS cloud credits and track-sponsor prize pools
Top 5 teams pitch on the SuperAI main stage
Visa invitation letters available for accepted internationals

name: TOKEN2049 NEXUS Startup Competition
prize: Equity-free prizes; investor exposure; global stage at world's largest crypto event
summary: NEXUS is the pitching arm of TOKEN2049, the world's largest crypto event. 500+ Web3 startups apply, 10 finalists pitch live to investors and the global crypto community over two days. Equity-free prizes plus the kind of investor exposure that has historically minted unicorns. Co-located with TOKEN2049 Origins, a 36-hour hackathon billed as 'crypto's premier hackathon.'
eligibility: Web3 startups; application-based selection from ~500 applicants to 10 finalists.
highlights: Direct exposure to top crypto VCs and founders
Co-located with the TOKEN2049 Origins hackathon

name: Antler Singapore Residency
prize: US$150K for 10% equity (US$100K + US$50K SAFE) + global network
summary: An inception-stage VC residency that compresses six months of company-building into six weeks. Antler invests in people from day zero — find a co-founder in the room, validate an idea, build, then take the cheque. Acceptance is under 3%. Graduates have raised down-stream from a thousands-strong Antler partner network. Backed by hands-on partners who've built and scaled themselves.
eligibility: Pre-team and pre-product founders globally. <3% acceptance.
highlights: US$150K cheque for 10% equity at residency end
Continuous follow-on investment up to Series C

name: RAISE Summit Hackathon & STAKES 2026
prize: €200,000 hackathon prize pool + €10M+ funding via STAKES startup contest
summary: Billed as the world's largest AI hackathon — 8,000 participants, 24 hours, €200,000 in prizes, and four tracks (Foundation, Frontier, Friction, Future) that span infrastructure, advanced apps, adoption barriers, and frontier AI. Co-located with RAISE the STAKES, the startup pitch competition that narrows 1,500+ applicants to 10 finalists pitching at Le Carrousel du Louvre. Apply by 1 June for STAKES; hackathon registration ongoing.
eligibility: Open globally. Hackathon: anyone. STAKES: AI-central startups, ≤€10M raised, pre-seed → Series A.
highlights: Co-located startup contest with €10M+ in funding
Held at Le Carrousel du Louvre, Paris

name: DevNetwork AI + ML Hackathon 2026
prize: Amazon Echos + DevNetwork Premium passes + sponsor prizes
summary: Co-located with AI DevSummit, this 2.5-week hybrid hackathon attracts 300+ developers building mobile, consumer-web, and business-web AI apps. Two-round judging: a generalist round on progress/concept/feasibility, then a sponsor-specific round where each sponsor picks their own challenge winner. Top 5 present on the AI DevSummit main expo stage on May 28. Submissions are public-domain — they're explicit about not being the place to launch a stealthy startup.
eligibility: Open globally. Solo or team. Devpost-based registration.
highlights: Top 5 present on the AI DevSummit Expo main stage
Public-domain ideas — not for stealth-mode pitches

name: Hack&Roll 2026
prize: Themed prize categories including 'most ridiculous language stack'
summary: Singapore's largest student-run hackathon. No themes, no limits — students from primary school through graduate studies build whatever they want over a continuous 24h venue. The vibe is intentionally chaotic and joyful: past winners include a Chrome extension that slices browsing images into puzzle pieces and a CV-powered seat-finder that detects 'choped' tissue-pack reservations.
eligibility: Open to all student levels (primary → graduate). Teams 1–4. Free.
highlights: Open 24 hours with shared rest area
Whimsical category prizes encourage creative experimentation

name: DEF CON Singapore 2026
prize: Contest-specific (Capture-the-Flag, Hacker Jeopardy, Villages)
summary: The first-ever DEF CON in Asia. Co-organised by HTX (Home Team Science & Technology Agency) and CSIT (Centre for Strategic Infocomm Technologies), it brings the legendary US security conference's contests, Villages, and 2-day pre-conference training tracks to Singapore. Expect Capture-the-Flag, hardware hacking, lockpicking villages, and unfiltered talks — DEF CON's stated mission is 'discovery,' not vendor pitches.
eligibility: Open registration. Early bird closed Feb 15. Student discount available.
highlights: Inaugural Asia edition of DEF CON
2-day technical training Apr 26–27 before main con
CTFs, Villages, and the iconic Hacker Jeopardy
Government-backed: HTX co-organising, CSIT strategic partner

name: Build for Impact Hackathon (AAAI 2026)
location: Singapore (during AAAI 2026 + SG AI Research Week)
prize: Mentorship and showcase opportunities with founding sponsors
summary: A 2-day in-person AI innovation hackathon held during the 40th AAAI Conference and Singapore AI Research Week. Participants apply design thinking to ideate and prototype around three impact tracks — Charity, Sustainability, Wellness — using SEA-LION, the local LLM developed by AI Singapore. Speakers and mentors are drawn from OpenAI, Google, AWS, Cloudflare, and Stanford.
eligibility: Open registration; designed for builders and applied researchers.
highlights: Use SEA-LION, AI Singapore's local LLM
Direct mentorship from OpenAI, Google, AWS, Cloudflare
Embedded inside AAAI — Singapore's biggest AI research week
Three impact tracks: Charity, Sustainability, Wellness

name: SBCC × Africa Challenge 2026
prize: S$1,500 + all-expenses-paid trip to Ghana for champions
summary: An NTU-run case competition asking student teams to design Africa-market expansion strategies for Singapore-based companies. The 2026 edition tasked teams with helping a cold-chain logistics firm enter West Africa's pharmaceutical sector — the winning team proposed an asset-light, tech-enabled, locally-adapted ecosystem. 130+ teams, 435 participants, 5 grand finalists.
eligibility: Singapore tertiary students (NUS, NTU, SMU, etc.).
highlights: Real Africa-market case from sponsor Olam Agri
Champions visit Ghana on a sponsored trip
Backed by NTU-SBF Centre for African Studies

name: Hack for Public Good 2026
prize: Selected projects continue as funded products at GovTech
summary: An annual hackathon by Open Government Products that brings every OGP officer together to ship projects targeting Singaporean public-service problems. Unusually for a hackathon, the work doesn't stop at demo day — over five years, hackathon products have been continuously developed and deployed into production government services. Participation is internal (OGP/GovTech officers) but the project portfolio and post-hackathon roadmap are public.
eligibility: Internal to Open Government Products officers.
highlights: Run by Singapore's Open Government Products team
Projects continue as funded products post-event
Five-year track record of shipped public services

name: Black Hat Asia 2026
location: Marina Bay Sands Expo & Convention Centre, Singapore
prize: Talk acceptance + research community recognition (no cash)
summary: The Asian arm of the Black Hat conference series — focused this year on the privacy/automation/AI-driven-security intersection, with keynotes from investigative journalist Violet Blue and others. Where DEF CON SG (a week later) is contests and villages, Black Hat is research talks, threat-intel briefings, and policy. Dual-purpose for security professionals: Briefings (talks) and Trainings (multi-day technical workshops).
eligibility: Open registration. CFP closed.
highlights: Long-running APAC arm of the Black Hat series
Held one week before DEF CON Singapore
Strong APAC government and enterprise audience

name: NUS Fintech Summit Hackathon 2026
prize: Top prize + 2,000 USDT BGA bounty for sustainability projects
summary: A 5-day fintech hackathon hosted by the NUS Fintech Society, single-tracked around Ripple's problem statement on blockchain-enabled financial applications. Participants attend physical workshops on the 5th-6th, then build for 48 hours. The Blockchain for Good Alliance offers a separate sustainability bounty for projects aligned with UN SDGs.
eligibility: University and tertiary students (teams of 3–5).
highlights: Single-track focus on XRPL / Ripple blockchain infrastructure
BGA bounty (up to 2,000 USDT) for SDG-aligned projects
Networking afterparty + career booth fest with sponsors

name: NTU Beyond Binary Techathon 2026
prize: S$10,000 total prize pool
summary: NTU Women in Tech's flagship inclusive techathon, with parallel Hackathon (build & prototype) and Ideathon (ideation & pitching) tracks. Designed to welcome students of all technical backgrounds — whether you code full-stack or craft strategy. Includes industry-led workshops with engineers from Jane Street, IMDA, Squarepoint, AWS, and Powers.
eligibility: Students from local Singapore universities (all genders welcome despite the name).
highlights: Workshops with Jane Street, IMDA, AWS

name: Manus AI For All Create-A-Thon
prize: S$20,000 prize pool + 2M Manus AI credits
summary: A one-day hackathon at NTU where students used 2M Manus AI credits to build real AI solutions for SMBs and freelancers. The challenge: democratize access to AI for businesses without ML teams. Founders, mentors, and driven students from across Singapore networked, then built fast on the Manus platform.
eligibility: Students across Singapore universities.
highlights: 2M free Manus AI credits per team
SMB & freelancer focus — real customers ready

name: NTU × Harvard HSIL Hackathon 2026
organizer: NTU School of Mechanical & Aerospace Engineering
prize: Recognition + venture incubation pathway
summary: NTU is the Singapore hub for Harvard's flagship Health Systems Innovation Lab (HSIL) Hackathon — a globally distributed event with hubs across five continents. The 2026 theme: 'Building High-Value Health Systems: Leveraging AI'. Selected teams enter an 8-week remote venture incubation program post-hackathon.
eligibility: Students, clinicians, engineers, designers, policy analysts, entrepreneurs.
highlights: Singapore hub of Harvard's global hackathon
8-week venture incubation program for top teams
Built around real health systems challenges

name: Hack&Roll 2026
prize: Custom HACK&ROLL badge (ESP32C3 + LEDs + buzzer) for every hacker
summary: Singapore's largest student-run hackathon — 24 hours of pure hacking joy. The vibe is wholesome chaos: hardware badges, weird hacks, overnight hacking. Welcomes everyone from primary school to grad students (though only undergrads can win prizes). International participants welcome but no visa/accommodation help.
eligibility: All students welcome (P1 → grad). Teams of 1–4. Prizes only for undergraduates.
highlights: Custom ESP32C3 hardware badge for everyone
Wholesome chaos energy — welcomes weird hacks

name: TreeHacks 2026 (Stanford)
prize: $1,030,875+ in prizes — including iPhone 17 Pro Max, Meta Ray-Bans, OpenAI internships
summary: The largest collegiate hackathon in the US. Stanford's invite-only event for college students, featuring over $1M in prizes. OpenAI offers winners a year of ChatGPT Pro + Michelin-star dinner + guaranteed interview. Heavy competition — ages 18+ only.
eligibility: Invited TreeHacks participants. Ages 18+. College students only.
highlights: OpenAI guaranteed interview for top winners
Hardware prizes per team member (iPhones, Meta Ray-Bans)

name: HackPrinceton Spring 2026
prize: Multiple track prizes including Wireless Earbuds, JBL Speakers, M5GO IoT Kits
summary: Princeton's premier hackathon, open to all college students globally. 36 hours of hacking with workshops, mentorship, and category prizes (Healthcare, Sustainability, Hardware, Games, Best First-Time Hack). Welcomes first-time hackers explicitly.
eligibility: College students from any university. Beginners explicitly welcome.
highlights: Best First-Time Hacker prize — beginners encouraged

name: Princeton Open Hackathon 2026 (NVIDIA GPU)
prize: GPU mentorship + NVIDIA expert support
summary: Princeton's annual GPU hackathon, hybrid format with virtual Day 0/Day 1 and hybrid Days 2–4. Sponsored by Oak Ridge National Lab + NVIDIA. Participants work alongside mentors from industry and national labs to migrate or optimize code for GPUs. Focused on real research codebases — bring your own.
eligibility: Researchers + students with computational research projects. Application required.
highlights: NVIDIA + Oak Ridge National Lab partnership
Bring your own real research code

name: UC Berkeley AI Hackathon 2026
prize: Cash prizes + tech prizes
summary: Berkeley's flagship AI hackathon — 2 days, fully in-person. Tracks include Science & Engineering (health tech, biotech, hardware), Social Impact (EdTech, civic platforms), and Open (games, generative art, experimental). Hardware rental booth with Arduinos, Raspberry Pis. No travel reimbursement.
eligibility: Apply via hive.hackberkeley.org. Priority deadline April 26; regular May 17.
highlights: Three tracks: Science/Engineering, Social Impact, Open

name: MLH 2026 Hackathon Season (200+ events)
prize: Varies per event — MLH Best Domain.com, GCP, etc.
summary: The official collegiate hackathon league, running over 200 student hackathons globally July 2025–June 2026. Find an event near you on the season schedule. 1 in 3 CS graduates has attended an MLH event. Free for students. New 2026 mascot: Jewel the Jumper Wire Jellyfish.
eligibility: Students at all education levels (middle school → grad school). 80% must be students.
highlights: Free for students at all levels
Solana Micro-Grant Program: 75 grants up to Dec 2026
MLH Software Lab: free APIs + dev tool credits

name: MLH Global Hack Week
prize: MLH XP points + season community recognition
summary: Online week-long themed hack events run by MLH throughout the season. Recent themes: Season Launch, AI/ML, Game Dev, Hardware, Web Dev. Earn XP by completing daily challenges (social, technical, design). Beginner-friendly — point-based, not prize-based.
eligibility: Anyone — sign up free on events.mlh.io.
highlights: Daily challenge format (do as many as you want)

name: ETHGlobal Cannes 2026
prize: Sponsor-backed prize pool across DeFi, ZK, L2 tracks
summary: ETHGlobal's spring 2026 stop in Cannes. Three days of in-person hacking on zero-knowledge, DeFi, L2s, privacy, and interoperability. Beginner-friendly yet deeply technical — hundreds of devs from around the world.
eligibility: Apply via ETHGlobal.com. Mix of Web3 vets + Web2 builders crossing over.
highlights: 3 days in Cannes Palace of Festivals

name: ETHGlobal New York 2026
prize: Sponsor-backed prize pool across AI x Crypto, ZK, DeFi tracks
summary: ETHGlobal's flagship NYC event. Three-day Web3 hackathon focused on AI x Crypto, ZK Proofs, and DeFi. Builders from around the world; beginner-friendly with curated content + free food. One of the highest-energy hackathons of the year.
eligibility: Apply via ETHGlobal.com. Open globally.

name: ETHGlobal Lisbon 2026
prize: Sponsor-backed prize pool
summary: Part of Onchain Lisbon — a major Web3 ecosystem week in Portugal. 36 continuous hours of hacking, Friday 9pm to Sunday 9am. Pragma Lisbon (June 8) is a separate one-day summit, but ETHGlobal Lisbon is the 36-hour build event.
eligibility: Apply via ETHGlobal.com.
highlights: Part of Onchain Lisbon ecosystem week

name: ETHGlobal Tokyo 2026
prize: Sponsor-backed prize pool
summary: ETHGlobal's Tokyo stop. 800+ builders gathered for 3 days of hacking, mentorship, and ecosystem exposure. Designed for both Web3 veterans and Web2 devs crossing over. Tracks span DeFi, infra, identity, gaming, NFTs, L2 scaling.
eligibility: Apply via ETHGlobal.com.
highlights: Tracks: DeFi, infra, identity, gaming, NFTs

name: ETHGlobal Mumbai 2026
prize: Sponsor-backed prize pool across DeFi, Privacy, AI tracks
summary: ETHGlobal's first major Indian event. Day 1 is a full convention with talks, panels, sponsor activations. Days 2–4 are the high-energy 36-hour hackathon with tracks in DeFi, Privacy, and AI. Bridges the global ETHGlobal circuit with India's massive developer community.
eligibility: Apply via ETHGlobal.com.

name: AMD x LabLab.ai AI Stack Hackathon
prize: $21,500+ total prize pool + 1 × AMD Radeon AI PRO R9700 GPU
summary: Build AI hackathon projects on AMD MI300X GPUs with $100 in free credits, ROCm open-source stack, and free courses. 7 days online to ship a working AI prototype. On-site invite to MindsDB SF AI Collective May 9–10 (no travel reimbursement).
eligibility: Open globally. Sign up for AMD AI Developer Program to unlock credits.
highlights: $100 AMD Developer Cloud credits per participant

name: IBM Bob Hackathon (LabLab.ai)
prize: $10,000 prize pool
summary: 48-hour fully online hackathon to build with IBM Bob — an AI-powered development partner that reads your codebase and accelerates multi-step work. Build tools and workflows that real developers would use. Designed for builders new to AI tools too.
eligibility: Open globally. Sign up on LabLab.ai + Discord.

name: AI Agent Olympics — Milan AI Week
prize: $28,000+ prize pool
summary: Build the next generation of autonomous agents during Europe's largest AI conference (700+ speakers, 25,000 attendees). Build with Kraken CLI for tokenized US stocks. Showcase + awards on May 20.
eligibility: Open globally.
highlights: Hosted at Europe's largest AI event
Build autonomous trading agents with Kraken CLI

name: AI Genesis — Dubai (LabLab.ai)
prize: Live on-stage pitching + winners at /function1 AI Conference
summary: Hybrid global hackathon culminating live at /function1 AI Conference & Exhibition 2026 in Dubai. Online build phase Oct 26–Nov 2; selected participants invited to on-site build day; live pitches Nov 3. Travel not covered.
eligibility: Open globally. Apply for on-site experience separately.

name: Y Combinator Summer 2026 Batch
prize: $500,000 investment for 7% equity
summary: The world's most prestigious startup accelerator. Spring batch already passed (Feb 9 deadline). Summer 2026 batch deadline is May 4, 2026 at 8pm PT — apply online and get a decision by June 5. In-person batch in San Francisco.
eligibility: Founders globally. Idea-stage to revenue. Solo or team. Must commit full-time.

name: Y Combinator Early Decision (for Students)
prize: $500K investment + reserved spot in future batch
summary: Apply to YC while still in school and reserve a spot in a future batch. Get funded immediately and held until you graduate. Designed for students who want to finish degrees first. Even if undecided, apply — this is a free option.
eligibility: Currently enrolled students with startup ideas.
highlights: Reserved batch spot for after school

name: NASA ORBIT Challenge 2026
organizer: NASA Center of Excellence for Collaborative Innovation
prize: $380,000 in total prizes
summary: Multi-phase, student-focused challenge for the next generation of innovators, engineers, entrepreneurs, researchers. Compete for cash prizes + NASA expert mentorship + present at in-person showcase. Finalists join an exclusive accelerator for STEM and entrepreneurship careers.
eligibility: Students. Multi-phase application.

name: Microsoft Imagine Cup 2026
prize: Scale Path: $100,000 USD; Launch Path: $50,000 USD
summary: Microsoft's flagship student startup competition. Two paths: Scale (for startups with traction, up to $5M raised) and Launch (early-stage, no $100K+ funding). Build with Microsoft AI services. World Championship features mentoring with Satya Nadella for the winner. 2026 World Finalists already announced.
eligibility: Enrolled high-school or college/university students 18+.
highlights: Mentoring with Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella
Past alumni went on to Series A+

name: Google Solution Challenge 2026 — Build with AI
prize: ₹10,00,000+ prize pool (India), $3,000/member globally
summary: Google's annual student hackathon for university students globally. 2026 theme: 'Build with AI' — use Gemini, Vertex AI, Flutter, Firebase, Google Cloud to solve UN SDG challenges. India track has prize pool of ₹10,00,000+. Top 3 globally win $3,000/member + Google mentorship.
eligibility: GDG on Campus members, currently enrolled university students 18+.
highlights: Featured in Google Developers Blog if you win

name: Google Girl Hackathon 2026
prize: Internships + full-time interview opportunities at Google
summary: Google's exclusively female coding competition for India-based engineering students graduating 2026–2029. Multi-stage virtual format. Top performers get direct Google interviews, internship offers, full-time roles. Indian nationals only.
eligibility: Female students, Indian nationals, batches 2026–2029, engineering CS/IT/AI/DS.

name: Smart India Hackathon (SIH) 2026
prize: ₹1.5 lakh per category + nation-building recognition
summary: World's largest open innovation movement. Last edition: 5M+ students across 35+ locations. Solve problem statements directly from government ministries, PSUs, industries, and NGOs. 36-hour grand finale at distributed nodal centers. Winning solutions get adopted by government departments.
eligibility: Students from Indian engineering colleges. Software + Hardware editions.
highlights: Winning solutions adopted at national level

name: India Innovates 2026 — National Civic Tech Hackathon
organizer: HN x Municipal Corporation of Delhi
date: Submissions until March 28, 2026; Finale at Bharat Mandapam
summary: World's largest civic tech hackathon. Aligned with Viksit Bharat 2047 vision. Build for governance, urban systems, democracy, cyber security, national infrastructure. NOT a build-on-site hackathon — development must be complete before March 28; finale day is for live demos at Bharat Mandapam in front of investors, government officials, diplomats.
eligibility: Indian student teams of 3–6.
highlights: Demo at iconic Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi

name: GirlCode 2026 — ADA's Women AI Hackathon
prize: Travel & accommodation for top 5 to ADA India office
summary: ADA's flagship women-only AI hackathon for Indian university students. Entirely online with finalists pitching live at ADA India office (travel + accommodation covered). Open to female university students of any major + any coding experience level.
eligibility: Female university students in India. Any major, any coding level.
highlights: Top 5 travel + accommodation covered to ADA India

name: Smart Horizon — 48hr International Hackathon (NHCE)
prize: TA reimbursement up to ₹10,000 per Indian team
summary: International 48-hour hackathon at NHCE Bangalore. Domains: AI for social good, sustainability, smart cities, health-tech, digital governance, cybersecurity. Elite advisory committee with experts from space tech, government, academia. International + Indian participants.
eligibility: Student teams + researchers + faculty + industry mentors.
highlights: TA reimbursement up to ₹10K per Indian team
Elite advisory committee from industry + academia
Aligned with Viksit Bharat / Atmanirbhar Bharat

name: Harvard HSIL Hackathon 2026 (Global)
organizer: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health — HSIL
date: Application Feb 9 – March 6, 2026; Hackathon Feb 9 – March 6 hub program
location: Multi-hub (5 continents) including Cambridge UK, Hanoi, Singapore, Sydney
prize: 8-week Venture Building Program + recognition
summary: Annual flagship Harvard hackathon with 2026 theme: 'Building High-Value Health Systems: Leveraging AI'. Distributed across 5 continents — Cambridge, Hanoi Medical University, UNSW, Westmead, NTU Singapore, and more. Selected teams enter 8-week remote Venture Incubation Program post-hackathon.
eligibility: Students, clinicians, engineers, designers, policy analysts, entrepreneurs.
highlights: Apply via local hub (e.g., NTU Singapore)
8-week Venture Building Program for top teams
Theme: AI for high-value health systems

name: MedHack: Frontiers Melbourne 2026
prize: Cash + tech prizes across Small + Big Tract
summary: MLAI's healthcare AI hackathon. Two tracks: Small (coding) for hands-on AI development, Big (pitching) for ideation. Continuous patient vitals data provided for the coding tract. Mentors: doctors, nurses, pharmacists, industry mentors. Beginner-friendly; team formation event the day before.
eligibility: All skill levels welcome. Australia-based or remote teams via in-person team member.
highlights: Team formation event for solo attendees

name: Boston University MedAI Hackathon
organizer: BU Faculty of Computing & Data Sciences
prize: $10,000 prize pool
summary: BU's first MedAI Hackathon: 233 students across 30 teams competing for $10K. Three challenge tracks: early lung cancer detection, Alzheimer's marker quantification, kidney injury prediction. Real de-identified data from active research labs. Cross-disciplinary teams from medicine, business, engineering, arts.
eligibility: BU students (may expand to other Boston universities in future).
highlights: Real research data from active BU labs

name: Georgetown H2AI — Healthcare Hackathon with AI
prize: Apple AirPods 4 (Audience Choice) + main prizes
summary: 3-day in-person hackathon featuring clinicians, engineers, business leaders, students. 150+ participants from CS, math, medicine, business teaming up on real clinical challenges (Parkinson's monitoring, schedule optimization, mental health AI). FREE to attend; recent grads (within 5 years) eligible.
eligibility: Undergrads, grads, recent grads (within 5 years). All experience levels welcome.
highlights: Open to recent grads up to 5 years out
30 teams from MIT, Hopkins, CMU, Cornell, Penn

name: Cornell Health Tech Hackathon 2026
prize: Tech + cash prizes
summary: Interdisciplinary in-person event for curious, innovative young people. Tracks include AI-powered XR healthcare (Meta Quest, Apple Vision Pro, HoloLens 2 available on-site), voice biomarkers for disease detection, and embodied clinical experiences. Bring teams from any major.
eligibility: Students from across degrees, majors, schools.
highlights: XR hardware available on-site (Vision Pro, Quest, HoloLens)

name: Wilkes Climate Solutions Hackathon
organizer: Wilkes Center for Climate Science & Policy
location: University of Utah + UofU Asia Campus, Korea
prize: $1,000–$5,000 in cash prizes
summary: 24-hour climate solutions hackathon — open to ALL disciplines, no coding required. Build slide-deck proposals to climate/energy challenges. Themes have included wildland fire, urban heat, water resources. Now expanded internationally to Incheon, South Korea (April 2026). Work in interdisciplinary teams of 3–5.
eligibility: Undergraduate + graduate students, any discipline.
highlights: Any discipline welcome — no coding required

name: SpaceHACK for Sustainability 2026
organizer: ASU Library Unit for Data Science & Analytics
prize: Cash prizes + workshops
summary: Annual two-day hackathon using satellite data to tackle global sustainability challenges. Recent tracks: mangrove flood protection in Guayaquil, decarbonizing global trade green corridors, monitoring Alpine glaciers. No prior satellite-data experience required. In-person + virtual options.
eligibility: University and community college students globally.

name: Earth Hacks 2026
prize: Varies per event — often partnerships + scholarships
summary: Earth Hacks works with college students + organizations to host environmental hackathons focused on creating innovative, equitable, and just solutions to the climate crisis. Multiple events throughout 2026 — find one near you.
eligibility: College students globally.

name: MIT Energy & Climate Hack 2026
prize: Cash prizes + investor connections
summary: MIT's flagship climate hack focused on renewable energy, sustainable transportation, circular economy, sustainable land use. Build solutions for clean energy + reducing carbon footprints. High-quality MIT mentor pool.
eligibility: Students globally.

name: Women Techmakers She Builds AI
prize: Featured in Google promotions + recognition
summary: Google Women Techmakers' global hackathon to address UN SDG 5 (Gender Equality) using AI. Build with Google Gemini or Gemma. Open to ALL genders despite the framing — built around addressing gender equity challenges. Free trainings + events leading up to the hackathon.
eligibility: Open to all genders (despite the framing).
highlights: Open to all genders (gender equity is the topic)

name: ASU Women in Computer Science x Opportunity Hack Spring 2026
prize: Cash + monitor for each team member; ElevenLabs Scale tier (6 months) per team member
summary: ASU WiCS partners with Opportunity Hack to solve real nonprofit problems. Two tracks: World Institute for Action Learning + Client Case Management. Open to all university students (you don't need to be at ASU or in WiCS). In-person only.
eligibility: Any university student (undergrad or grad). Don't need to be in WiCS.

name: HSBC Technology Hackathon 2026 (India)
prize: Internships + Pre-Placement Offers + certifications
summary: HSBC's 12-hour online coding challenge for students graduating in 2026 with B.E./B.Tech in CS or IT. Topics: AI/ML, Cryptography, Data Science, API/Microservice Engineering. Min 70% / 7 CGPA throughout. May lead to 4–6 month internship + PPO opportunity.
eligibility: B.E./B.Tech CS/IT students graduating 2026, India, min 70% / 7 CGPA.

name: Techex Intelligent Enterprise Solutions Hackathon
location: Online + San Jose McEnery Convention Center
prize: $10,000 prize pool
summary: Build enterprise-ready AI solutions during AI & Big Data Expo North America. Tracks: AI agents, RAG over enterprise data, AI security, Gemini-powered workflows, robotics + simulation, vision-language models. Winners announced live on stage May 19.
eligibility: Open globally. Solo or team.
highlights: Live winners announcement at AI & Big Data Expo NA

name: NEXT Hackathon @ SuperAI Singapore 2026
prize: AWS credits + Free SuperAI conference access (US$999) + spotlight demos
summary: 200 builders for a 36-hour sprint at Asia's largest AI conference. Application-based admission — every person in the room is here to ship. Top 5 teams demo on the SuperAI stage. 100% free including food, drinks, and full SuperAI conference access. Visa support letters available for accepted internationals.
eligibility: Application-based. Early/growth-stage startups, enterprise dev engineers, students, researchers. Max 4 per team. Solo OK.
highlights: Visa letters provided for accepted internationals

name: Complete AI Hackathon (LabLab.ai)
prize: $3,500 in prizes + premium 3-month Complete.dev access
summary: 342 builders from 63+ countries used collaborative AI agents to design + build complete AI products in one week. Teams work alongside multiple AI agents in shared workspaces. Focus on real-world products that teams could actually ship. Prior recap: Complete.dev community of shippers.
eligibility: Open globally.
highlights: AI agents as collaborators, not just tools

name: LabLab Next: Create Your Startup
prize: GAIA Startup Accelerator entry for victors
summary: 10-day startup-launching hackathon. Take your AI prototype to MVP stage; collaborate with peers globally. Hackathon winners enter GAIA Startup Accelerator. Designed for builders who already have prototypes from earlier hackathons.
eligibility: Open globally. Best for those with existing prototypes.

name: Imagine Cup Singapore National Final
prize: National finalist support + path to global Imagine Cup
summary: Microsoft Singapore's national track for the Imagine Cup. Singapore-based student teams compete locally before the global finals. Build with Microsoft AI services. Winners advance to global rounds for $50K-$100K prizes.
eligibility: Singapore-based student teams.

name: MLH Global Hack Week — Season Launch
prize: MLH XP points + season recognition
summary: Celebrates the launch of MLH's hackathon season with special community announcements. XP earned for completing challenges (social, technical, design) — the more you do, the more you accumulate. Beginner-friendly daily challenges range from social media posts to building full demos.
eligibility: Anyone — sign up free on events.mlh.io.

name: Anthropic Fellows Programs (general)
prize: Paid fellowship + research/engineering access at Anthropic
summary: Anthropic runs research engineer + safety fellowship cohorts year-round (e.g., the Anthropic Research Fellows program for AI safety researchers). Pathway from external researcher to full-time hire. Check Anthropic careers page for current listings.
eligibility: Varies per fellowship — research, engineering, safety.
highlights: Direct access to frontier AI research

name: OpenAI Residency Program
prize: Paid residency salary + pathway to full-time at OpenAI
summary: OpenAI's flagship residency program for engineers and researchers transitioning into AI. Six-month paid program with full access to OpenAI's compute, data, and research community. Designed for those without traditional ML PhDs but with strong engineering or research backgrounds in adjacent fields.
eligibility: Strong engineering or research background; no ML PhD required.
highlights: Pathway for non-ML PhDs into frontier AI

name: Knight-Hennessy Scholars (Stanford)
prize: Full funding for any Stanford grad program (3 years)
summary: Stanford's flagship graduate fellowship — fully funded for ANY Stanford graduate program. Builds a multidisciplinary cohort of leaders. Open globally, all majors. Highly competitive but applications welcome from any background — engineers, doctors, artists, policy folk.
eligibility: Applying to any Stanford graduate program. Bachelor's in last 7 years.
highlights: Full funding for ANY Stanford grad program
Highest-prestige US fellowship for grad school

name: Rhodes Scholarship
date: Annual (deadlines vary by country, typically Aug–Oct)
prize: Full funding for graduate study at Oxford
summary: The world's oldest international graduate fellowship. Up to 100 scholars per year selected from 60+ countries. Funds graduate study at Oxford. Beyond academic merit — looks for character, leadership, commitment to service. Singapore is one of the eligible jurisdictions.
eligibility: Citizenship requirements vary by jurisdiction. Apply to your home country selection.

name: Thiel Fellowship
prize: $100,000 grant to skip school + 2 years of mentorship
summary: Pays exceptional young people ($100K) to skip or pause college and build something. Famously: Vitalik Buterin (Ethereum), Austin Russell (Luminar), Laura Deming (longevity). Under 23. Apply with a working idea, a track record, and a genuine willingness to leave the academic path.
eligibility: Under 23. Any country. Must commit to NOT being in school during the fellowship.

name: Z Fellows (Peter Thiel x Z Combinator)
prize: $10,000 + 1-week intensive in SF
summary: Cory Levy's program: $10K + a focused week in SF for the most ambitious young builders. Designed to be the on-ramp from doing-well to building-something. Alumni go on to YC, raise rounds. Under 25 typically.
eligibility: Young builders. Apply via z-fellows.com.

name: a16z Talent x Opportunity Fund
prize: Up to $175K investment + a16z mentorship
summary: Founded specifically to back founders without traditional access to venture capital. Funds early-stage builders who are talented but lack the typical Stanford/MIT pedigree. Up to $175K + access to the a16z network.
eligibility: Founders without traditional VC access. Rolling.

name: Singapore FinTech Festival 2026 — Global FinTech Hackcelerator
prize: S$50,000 cash prizes + investor connections
summary: MAS's flagship fintech accelerator running parallel to Singapore FinTech Festival (largest fintech event in the world). Submit your fintech solution; top 21 finalists get cash, investor access, and a booth at Singapore FinTech Festival in November.
eligibility: Fintech founders globally. Apply with working solution + corporate problem statement.
highlights: Pipeline to Singapore FinTech Festival (60K+ attendees)

name: Tech for Good Empowerment Programme — IMDA
prize: Funding + mentorship for Tech for Good initiatives
summary: IMDA's program supporting tech-for-good builders in Singapore. Funds projects addressing accessibility, mental health, climate, social inequality. Year-round application. Strong alignment with national Smart Nation agenda.
eligibility: Singapore-based founders + builders.

name: Google DeepMind Research Residency
prize: Paid residency + DeepMind network
summary: DeepMind's flagship residency for early-career researchers. Designed for those with strong research or engineering backgrounds outside traditional ML. Year-long program with mentorship from DeepMind scientists.
eligibility: Strong research background. Bachelor's+ required.

name: Kaggle Competitions (rolling)
prize: $10K – $1M+ per competition (varies)
summary: The world's largest data science competition platform. Multiple active competitions with prize pools from $10K to over $1M. Topics range from medical imaging to NLP to time series forecasting. Beginner tutorials abundant. Strong DSA + portfolio signal.
eligibility: Anyone with internet access.

name: Devpost Hackathons (browse hundreds)
prize: Varies — $100 to $1M+ per event
summary: Devpost is the largest online hackathon platform. Hundreds of active hackathons at any given time, from corporate-sponsored to student-led to single-week online sprints. Free signup. Best one-stop discovery for online hackathons globally.
eligibility: Anyone with a Devpost account.

name: Unstop Hackathons (India + Global)
prize: Varies — internships, jobs, cash prizes
summary: India's largest competition + hackathon platform with strong recruiter integrations. Hosts case competitions, hackathons, quizzes, and hiring contests. Many companies recruit directly from Unstop performers. Search by skill, company, prize.
eligibility: Anyone — strong India focus + global open events.

name: Devfolio Hackathons
prize: Varies — strong Web3 + fintech sponsorships
summary: India's leading hackathon platform with strong Web3, fintech, AI focus. Top-tier events with seamless registration, team collaboration, mentor support. Showcase projects + connect with potential employers/investors via the community.
eligibility: Anyone — strong India + global open.

name: Techstars Accelerator (multiple programs)
prize: $120,000 ($20K cash + $100K convertible note)
summary: One of the world's most active accelerators. 30+ programs annually across cities + verticals (climate, mobility, fintech, etc.). 13 weeks of mentorship + investment + Demo Day. Apply via specific program of interest.
eligibility: Founders globally. Stage: idea → revenue. 13-week commitment required.

name: Google for Startups Accelerator: Southeast Asia
prize: $200K Google Cloud credits + Google mentorship
summary: Google's flagship accelerator for ASEAN founders. 10-week program with mentorship from Google engineers + product leaders. Equity-free. $200K Google Cloud credits. Strong fit for AI-first startups.
eligibility: ASEAN-based startups, Series A or earlier.

name: Smart Nation Challenges (Singapore)
prize: Procurement opportunities + government contracts
summary: Government-backed open challenges for Singapore's Smart Nation initiative. Multiple problem statements across digital identity, healthcare, mobility, urban planning. Winners often secure government procurement contracts.
eligibility: Open to companies + individuals.

name: HackHarvard 2026
prize: Cash + tech prizes across multiple tracks
summary: Harvard's flagship student hackathon. 36 hours, Boston tech scene. Categories range from AI to social good to hardware. Strong sponsor lineup (recent: Google, Meta, Capital One). MLH-affiliated.
eligibility: College students globally. Apply via website.

name: HackMIT 2026
prize: Cash + tech prizes; corporate sponsor swag
summary: MIT's flagship undergraduate hackathon. ~1,000 hackers, 36 hours, Stanford-tier ambition. Tracks include AI, hardware (with extensive component lab), gaming, social impact. MIT Media Lab + EECS + Sloan presence.
eligibility: College students globally. Apply via portal.
highlights: Most prestigious US college hackathon (besides TreeHacks)

name: Hack the North 2026
prize: Cash + tech prizes
summary: Canada's largest hackathon — 1,000+ attendees. Notable for being explicitly inclusive: ~1/3 of attendees are first-time hackers. Travel reimbursement for accepted international hackers. Embraces 'radical welcome' philosophy.
eligibility: Students globally. Apply for travel reimbursement.

name: Cal Hacks 2026 (Berkeley)
prize: Cash + tech prizes across multiple tracks
summary: Berkeley's largest annual hackathon. 1,500+ hackers in past editions. Strong AI focus with Berkeley AI Lab presence. Multiple tracks: AI, hardware, social impact, web3. MLH-affiliated.
eligibility: Students globally.

name: SMU .Hack HEAP 2026 (Enrichment Application Programme)
prize: S$1,750 prize pool + mentorship and workshops with Google, AWS, Huawei
summary: SMU .Hack's flagship summer software-development immersion: students form teams and ship a self-chosen application across ten weeks with mentorship from SMU seniors, alumni, and industry. Weekly technical workshops cover full-stack, cloud, and AI patterns, plus on-site visits to partner companies. Beginner-friendly - first-time builders welcome.
eligibility: SMU students and invited external university students; open to all skill levels
highlights: 10-week build cycle with weekly technical workshops
Industry mentorship from Google, AWS, Huawei
Beginner-friendly: no prior project experience required

name: SLINGSHOT 2026 - Asia's Deep Tech Pitching Competition
prize: Grand prize S$400K Startup SG grant; runners-up S$300K / S$200K; each Top 10 gets S$80K
summary: The 10th edition of Asia's flagship deep-tech pitching competition, bringing global startups to Singapore for an immersion programme and live finals at SWITCH. Top 50 receive sponsored Singapore trips; Top 10 split nearly S$1M in non-dilutive Startup SG grant prizes. Domain finals cover AI, sustainability, healthcare, and frontier tech.
eligibility: Deep-tech startups globally (under 7 years old, pre-Series B); founders apply with a 10-slide pitch
highlights: S$400K grand prize (non-dilutive Startup SG grant)
Top 50 fly to Singapore on a sponsored immersion programme
Pitches in front of SWITCH 2026 investor audience

name: Google Cloud Rapid Agent Hackathon 2026
prize: US$50K+ prize pool - bucket system: 1st US$5K / 2nd US$3K / 3rd US$2K per partner bucket
summary: Google Cloud's open call to build agentic AI that reasons, plans, and executes real tasks - not just chatbots. Builders use Gemini 3 and Google Cloud's Agent Builder stack to ship within a six-week window. A bucket-prize system means more winners across multiple partner-defined categories rather than one winner-take-all track.
eligibility: Open globally; all skill levels welcome (Devpost-hosted, solo or team)
highlights: Built on Gemini 3 + Google Cloud Agent Builder
Bucket-prize structure spreads wins across categories

name: FIND EVIL! - SANS Autonomous Incident Response Hackathon
prize: US$22K+ pool: 1st US$10K + full SANS Summit pass per team member, 2nd US$7.5K, 3rd US$3K
summary: The first hackathon dedicated to building autonomous AI agents for cyber incident response. Teams extend Protocol SIFT - the framework that wires AI agents into SANS's SIFT Workstation tooling - into a self-driving IR agent. Winning code gets reviewed for upstream integration. Already 1,100+ registered builders.
eligibility: Solo or teams up to 5; no incident-response background required
highlights: 1st place: cash + full SANS Summit package for whole team
Winning code may merge into Protocol SIFT upstream
Judged on hallucination management + audit-trail quality

name: Build with MeDo Hackathon (Baidu)
prize: US$50K pool - 1st place US$10K plus multiple category prizes
summary: Baidu's global showcase for MeDo, its no-code full-stack AI builder. Describe what you want; MeDo generates the app, lets you iterate via chat or visual editor, and one-click deploys. The hackathon is deliberately beginner-friendly - the platform handles the engineering so submissions are judged on creativity and use-case fit. Closes 20 May - submit fast.
eligibility: Open globally to all skill levels; no coding background required
highlights: Truly no-code: build via natural-language description

name: AWS Prompt the Planet Challenge 2026
prize: US$50K in AWS credits split across 10 winners
summary: AWS's prompt-engineering challenge: submit prompts that help developers accomplish real AWS tasks (provisioning, debugging, cost optimisation, security review). Ten winners share US$50K in AWS credits. Multiple submissions are allowed, so iteration pays. A low-effort, high-leverage entry for anyone who already lives in the AWS console.
eligibility: Open globally; individual submissions; multiple entries allowed
highlights: Submit prompts, not full apps - extremely low entry friction
Ten winners share US$50K AWS credits

name: International AI Agents Hackathon 2026
prize: US$100 cash prize + bragging rights
summary: A low-stakes, fast online hackathon focused on AI agents for enterprise, ML, and web use cases. Closes 29 May. Prize pool is small but it's a clean three-week container to ship a working agent demo and get a Devpost portfolio piece. Good warm-up for the bigger Google Cloud Rapid Agent or FIND EVIL! tracks running in parallel.
eligibility: Open globally to all skill levels

name: 4th Singapore-India Hackathon 2026 (SMU)
organizer: Singapore Management University + Indian Ministry of Education
prize: Cross-border industry mentorship + government-backed recognition
summary: The fourth edition of the bilateral Singapore-India Hackathon, marking 60 years of diplomatic ties. University teams from both countries pair up to solve government-and-industry-defined challenges at SMU. Backed by both Education Ministries - Minister Desmond Lee opened the launch event alongside Infosys co-founder Narayana Murthy. Less prize-driven, more about cross-border collaboration credentials.
eligibility: University students from Singapore and India institutions; team formation includes both nationalities

name: Sea x OpenAI Codex Hackathon - Singapore (APAC kickoff)
location: Shopee Building, 5 Science Park Drive, Singapore 118265
prize: US$30K / US$15K / US$5K in OpenAI API credits + ChatGPT Pro subscriptions for top 5 teams
summary: Sea (parent of Shopee, Garena, SeaMoney) and OpenAI kick off their first regional Codex hackathon series in Singapore - a one-day live build at Shopee HQ with 150+ developers across three tracks: autonomous and adaptive AI, AI-native products and operations, and deep domain AI. This is not an intro workshop - the brief explicitly asks for builders already shipping production-grade code with AI coding tools. Wider APAC roadshow follows the Singapore kickoff.
eligibility: Developers, students, entrepreneurs comfortable using AI coding tools; teams of 3-4, each member applies individually
highlights: First stop of the new Sea x OpenAI APAC Codex hackathon series
US$30K top prize in OpenAI API credits (+ ChatGPT Pro for top 5)
One-day, in-person at Shopee HQ - 150+ builders, 40+ projects
Three live tracks: autonomous AI, AI-native ops, deep-domain AI

name: Build with Gemini XPRIZE - 90-Day AI Business Challenge
date: 19 May - 17 August 2026 (live finals 25 September 2026, Los Angeles)
location: Online build, finals at Los Angeles
prize: US$2,000,000 total: US$500K grand, US$200K 2nd, three US$100K runner-ups, 15 x US$50K runner-ups, 5 x US$50K category prizes
summary: XPRIZE's biggest-ever hackathon, announced by Google at I/O 2026: launch a real Gemini-powered business in 90 days, acquire real users, and generate real revenue. Five categories cover Education & Human Potential, Entrepreneurship & Jobs, Small Business Services, Money & Financial Access, and Professional Services. Judged on actual revenue earned, AI-native operations, and category impact - not slideware. Hacker Fund handles screening; five finalists pitch live in LA.
eligibility: Open globally to individuals or teams; must launch a real business with real users and revenue during the 90-day window
highlights: US$2M total prize pool - largest Gemini-focused hackathon ever
Judged on actual revenue + AI-native ops, not pitch quality
Hacker Fund-curated; finalists pitch live in LA on 25 Sept
Five category tracks - up to US$500K grand prize

name: USAII Global AI Hackathon 2026 - Virtual Student Competition
date: AI Readiness Qualifier 10 June 2026; build window 14-21 June 2026; results 12 July 2026
prize: USAII certifications, official recognition, trophy and merchandise (no cash track)
summary: A fully-virtual, student-only AI hackathon for high schoolers, undergrads, and grad students worldwide. Short qualifier on June 10 filters into a one-week build window. Less prize-driven than the corporate AI hackathons but a clean structured competition designed specifically for student builders - a useful portfolio piece and certification credential. Registration is free.
eligibility: Students worldwide in grades 9-12, undergraduate, graduate, or doctoral programs
highlights: Includes USAII certification path for finalists

name: DeveloperWeek New York 2026 Hackathon
date: 25 May - 10 June 2026 (kickoff 26 May; final submissions 10 June 10:00 AM EST)
location: Hybrid - TWA Hotel, Queens NYC + online
prize: US$20K+ in cash, products, and sponsor-track prizes
summary: DeveloperWeek New York's official hackathon - hybrid, open-tracks, sponsor-heavy. Build-anything format with sponsor challenges layered on top; two judging rounds (Overall + per-sponsor). New York-based builders converge at the iconic TWA Hotel for kickoff while remote teams join online. 300+ developers expected. Short window - submissions close 10 June.
eligibility: Open globally; solo or team; in-person or online participation both supported
highlights: Hybrid: in-person at TWA Hotel + online
Two judging rounds widen the prize surface
300+ developers - good networking for NYC tech
